sections.heading: TNM
sections.content:
AJCC Cancer Staging Manual, 8th edition (2017) TNM system for bladder cancer [19].

t_stages: [{"stage": "TX", "description": "Primary tumor cannot be assessed."}, {"stage": "T0", "description": "No evidence of primary tumor."}, {"stage": "Ta", "description": "Noninvasive papillary carcinoma."}, {"stage": "CIS (Tis)", "description": "Urothelial carcinoma in situ: 'flat tumor'."}, {"stage": "T1", "description": "Tumor invades lamina propria (subepithelial connective tissue)."}, {"stage": "T2", "description": "Tumor invades muscularis propria."}, {"stage": "T2a", "description": "Tumor invades superficial muscularis propria (inner half)."}, {"stage": "T2b", "description": "Tumor invades deep muscularis propria (outer half)."}, {"stage": "T3", "description": "Tumor invades perivesical tissue."}, {"stage": "T3a", "description": "Microscopically."}, {"stage": "T3b", "description": "Macroscopically (extravesical mass)."}, {"stage": "T4", "description": "Extravesical tumor directly invades adjacent structures."}, {"stage": "T4a", "description": "Invades prostatic stroma, seminal vesicles, uterus, or vagina."}, {"stage": "T4b", "description": "Invades pelvic wall or abdominal wall."}],

n_stages: [{"stage": "NX", "description": "Regional lymph nodes cannot be assessed."}, {"stage": "N0", "description": "No lymph node metastasis."}, {"stage": "N1", "description": "Single regional lymph node metastasis in the true pelvis (perivesical, obturator, internal and external iliac, or sacral)."}, {"stage": "N2", "description": "Multiple regional lymph node metastasis in the true pelvis."}, {"stage": "N3", "description": "Lymph node metastasis to the common iliac lymph nodes."}],

m_stages: [{"stage": "M0", "description": "No distant metastasis."}, {"stage": "M1", "description": "Distant metastasis."}, {"stage": "M1a", "description": "Distant metastasis limited to lymph nodes beyond the common iliacs."}, {"stage": "M1b", "description": "Non-lymph-node distant metastases."}],

stage_groups: [{"group": "Stage 0a", "criteria": "Ta, N0, M0", "clinical_meaning": "Noninvasive papillary carcinoma confined to the urothelium.", "five_yr_survival": "Excellent (>90%); treatment intent: curative with intravesical therapy.", "treatment_intent": "Curative; intravesical therapy and surveillance."}, {"group": "Stage 0is", "criteria": "CIS (Tis), N0, M0", "clinical_meaning": "Flat high-grade carcinoma in situ.", "five_yr_survival": "Excellent with treatment; risk of progression if untreated.", "treatment_intent": "Curative; intravesical BCG."}, {"group": "Stage I", "criteria": "T1, N0, M0", "clinical_meaning": "Tumor invades lamina propria.", "five_yr_survival": "~80-90%; risk of progression depends on grade and other features.", "treatment_intent": "Curative; TURBT ± intravesical therapy; consider cystectomy for high-risk features."}, {"group": "Stage II", "criteria": "T2a or T2b, N0, M0", "clinical_meaning": "Tumor invades muscularis propria.", "five_yr_survival": "~50-60%; requires definitive treatment.", "treatment_intent": "Curative; neoadjuvant chemotherapy followed by cystectomy or bladder-preserving chemoradiation."}, {"group": "Stage IIIA", "criteria": "T3a, T3b, T4a, or T1-T4a with N1, N0, M0", "clinical_meaning": "Tumor extends into perivesical fat or involves prostate/uterus/vagina, or with single pelvic lymph node metastasis.", "five_yr_survival": "~30-40%; aggressive multimodal therapy needed.", "treatment_intent": "Curative intent with neoadjuvant chemotherapy, cystectomy, and/or chemoradiation."}, {"group": "Stage IIIB", "criteria": "T1-T4a, N2 or N3, M0", "clinical_meaning": "Multiple regional or common iliac lymph node involvement.", "five_yr_survival": "~15-25%; systemic therapy and consolidation local therapy.", "treatment_intent": "Curative intent in selected cases with aggressive systemic therapy and local consolidation."}, {"group": "Stage IVA", "criteria": "T4b, any N, M0; or any T, any N, M1a", "clinical_meaning": "Locally advanced disease fixed to pelvic/abdominal wall or distant lymph node metastases beyond common iliacs.", "five_yr_survival": "<10%; palliative intent usually.", "treatment_intent": "Palliative; systemic therapy, possible consolidative local therapy in responders."}, {"group": "Stage IVB", "criteria": "Any T, any N, M1b", "clinical_meaning": "Distant visceral metastases.", "five_yr_survival": "<5%; palliative intent.", "treatment_intent": "Palliative; systemic therapy to prolong survival and maintain quality of life."}],

staging_pearls: ["Clinical stage (c) is based on EUA, TURBT, and imaging; pathologic stage (p) requires cystectomy and lymph node dissection.", "Approximately 42% of patients are upstaged at cystectomy, highlighting the limitations of clinical staging.", "pT1 sub-staging may have prognostic value but is not widely utilized.", "Presence of lymphovascular invasion, variant histology, and prostatic urethral involvement are high-risk features.", "The 8th edition subdiv

t_stages.description: Tumor invades lamina propria (subepithelial connective tissue).
Tumor invades superficial muscularis propria (inner half).
Tumor invades deep muscularis propria (outer half).
Extravesical tumor directly invades adjacent structures.
Invades prostatic stroma, seminal vesicles, uterus, or vagina.
Invades pelvic wall or abdominal wall.

n_stages.description: Regional lymph nodes cannot be assessed.
Single regional lymph node metastasis in the true pelvis (perivesical, obturator, internal and external iliac, or sacral).
Multiple regional lymph node metastasis in the true pelvis.
Lymph node metastasis to the common iliac lymph nodes.

m_stages.description: Distant metastasis limited to lymph nodes beyond the common iliacs.
